title: KotaConfig
description: KOTA configuration file (.kota/config.json). Project-level config overrides global (~/.kota/config.json); CLI flags override both.

model.description: Default model ID to use for agent sessions (e.g. "claude-sonnet-4-6").

editorModel.description: Model ID to use for editor-style operations (diff application, etc.).

maxTokens.description: Maximum output tokens per API call.

architect.description: Enable architect mode (two-pass plan-then-implement). Default: false.

thinking.description: Enable extended thinking for supported models. Default: false.

thinkingBudget.description: Token budget for extended thinking when `thinking` is enabled. Minimum: 1024.

verbose.description: Enable verbose log output. Default: false.

skipConfirmations.description: Skip all interactive confirmation prompts (non-interactive mode). Default: false.

autoEnable.description: Tool groups to auto-enable at session start (e.g. ["web", "code"]).

user.description: User profile — injected into system prompt for personalization.

name.description: Your name, used in the system prompt.

context.description: Free-form context about you and your work injected into the system prompt.

aliases.description: Prompt aliases — keys that expand into prefix text when starting a message.

reflection.description: Evaluate response quality before delivering (self-reflection step). Default: true.

guardrails.description: Risk classification and policy enforcement for tool calls.

policies.description: Policy per risk level. Keys: safe, moderate, dangerous.

toolOverrides.description: Per-tool policy overrides. Keys are tool names; values are policies.

extensions.description: Per-extension configuration. Keys are extension names; values are extension-specific settings.

oneOf.description: Subprocess extension communicating via stdio (KEMP protocol).

env.description: Environment variables to set for the subprocess.

oneOf.description: HTTP extension communicating via HTTP (KEMP protocol).

url.description: Base URL for the HTTP extension endpoint.

providers.description: Provider overrides. Keys are service types (e.g. "memory", "knowledge"); values are provider names.

modelProvider.description: Model provider configuration for non-Anthropic backends (OpenAI-compat, Ollama, etc.).

baseUrl.description: Base URL for the provider API.

modelTiers.description: Model tier mapping for adaptive routing.

fast.description: Model ID for the fast tier (low-latency, high-throughput tasks).

balanced.description: Model ID for the balanced tier (default for most tasks).

capable.description: Model ID for the capable tier (complex reasoning tasks).

agentModels.description: Per-agent model overrides. Keys are agent names (built-in or extension-contributed); values are model IDs.

webhooks.description: Per-workflow webhook secrets for POST /webhooks/:workflowName. Keep in .kota/config.json (gitignored).

approvalTtlMs.description: TTL for pending approval items in milliseconds. Default: 86400000 (24 hours).

dailyBudgetUsd.description: Maximum API spend per calendar day (UTC). Workflow dispatch pauses when exceeded.

runsGc.description: Run artifact retention policy for .kota/runs/. Applied when `kota workflow gc` is run.

retentionDays.description: Delete runs older than this many days. Default: 7.

minKeepPerWorkflow.description: Always keep at least this many recent runs per workflow. Default: 10.

noAuth.description: Disable bearer-token auth. For localhost-only dev use. Default: false (auth enabled).

format.description: Output format. "text" is human-readable (default); "json" emits newline-delimited JSON for log aggregators. Overridden by LOG_FORMAT env var.

shutdownGracePeriodMs.description: How long (ms) to wait for active workflow runs to complete before aborting them during shutdown. 0 = drain indefinitely. Default: 60000 (60s).

eventBufferSize.description: Number of recent SSE events to retain in the in-memory ring buffer for GET /api/events and SSE reconnect replay. Default: 500.

alertCooldownMs.description: Minimum milliseconds between failure alerts for the same workflow. Default: 0 (no cooldown — every failure fires an alert). Example: 300000 suppresses repeated alerts within 5 minutes.

policy.description: Guardrail policy: allow (proceed silently), confirm (ask user), deny (block), queue (add to approval queue).
